text.title: AI-Assisted Lessons-Learned Synthesis Failure Modes
text.summary: ≥3 antipatterns observed in the wild. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Synthesis cites no quotes; themes 'sound right' but trace to nothing.
antipattern.root-cause: LLM defaulted to summarisation; verbatim-citation rule wasn't enforced.
antipattern.fix: Reject themes lacking 2 quotes; run validate-lessons-learned-synthesis.py to grep each quote against the source doc.

antipattern.symptom: Latest retros dominate; older recurring lessons get demoted.
antipattern.root-cause: LLM weights vivid recent content heavier.
antipattern.fix: Equal-weight across the time window; ingestion script reports retro count per quarter and resamples evenly.

antipattern.symptom: Verbatim quote outs a specific project + lead; psychological safety breached.
antipattern.root-cause: No anonymization pass between extract and synthesis.
antipattern.fix: Anonymisation step: project names → roles; people names → roles. Human pass double-checks.

antipattern.symptom: Synthesis ends with 'we should improve X'; quarter passes; nothing done.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent generated vague owners; human pass didn't replace.
antipattern.fix: Force specific role; if no role exists to own, the action is a process gap that itself needs an owner.
